get.description: Return the full system status. Always HTTP 200 while the process can serve.
Return readiness status. 200 only when aggregate status is 'ok', else 503.

Return the app configuration with schema-driven masking for the given app key.

Secret fields are masked by type: any field declared ``SecretStr`` is replaced
by a masked placeholder; plain ``str`` fields are never masked by name.
The ``config_schema`` is fully inlined (no ``$ref`` nodes remain).

Masking needs the app's config schema. It comes from the running instance when the
app is active, otherwise from the app class if it has already been loaded. When no
schema can be obtained (a disabled app whose class was never loaded, or a class whose
schema generation fails), every string value is masked as a safe floor so no secret
leaks — the masked path is the only path.
Return the source code of the app file for the given app key.
Return recent log records from the database with optional filtering.

schema.title: Response Get Logs Api Logs Recent Get

put.description:
Change a logger's effective level at runtime without restarting.

The change takes effect immediately for both structlog and stdlib callers on that logger.

get.description:
Return all log records for a single execution, with retention-expired detection.

For UUIDv7 execution IDs, the embedded timestamp is extracted directly without a
database lookup. For historical UUIDv4 IDs, the database is queried to determine
whether retention has expired.

Returns 422 if the execution_id is not a valid UUID. Returns an empty record list
with ``retention_expired=True`` if logs have been purged by retention policy.

schema.description: App instance index. Defaults to 0. Multi-instance apps have indices 0..N-1.

get.description:
Return the complete Hassette configuration as a JSON schema plus current values.

``config_schema`` is the fully-inlined JSON schema derived from the
``HassetteConfig`` class (all ``$ref``/``$defs`` resolved server-side).
``config_values`` is the current configuration serialized to JSON with
any ``SecretStr`` field replaced by a masked placeholder — the plaintext
value is never sent over the wire. Every field and nested group is present;
nothing is omitted.

Health check for the telemetry database.

Runs a representative query against the unified ``executions`` table.
Returns 503 with ``degraded: true`` when the database is
unavailable; 200 with ``degraded: false`` when healthy.
Health strip metrics for a single app instance.

Job summaries for a single app instance, enriched with live heap data.

Live fields (``next_run``, ``fire_at``, ``jitter``) are joined
from the live scheduler heap by ``db_id``. On heap failure the DB rows are
returned without enrichment (degraded but functional; logged warning, no 500).

Combined execution list (handler invocations and job executions).

Filter by ``kind=handler`` or ``kind=job`` to restrict to one type.
Each record includes a ``kind`` field that discriminates the execution type.

get.description: Execution history for a specific listener (handler invocations).

schema.title: Response Job Executions Api Telemetry Job  Job Id  Executions Get

get.description:
Per-app health data for the dashboard grid.

Always uses ``source_tier='app'`` — framework actors are shown via FrameworkHealth,
not the manifest-driven app grid.

All scheduled jobs across all apps, enriched with live heap data.

Live fields (``next_run``, ``fire_at``, ``jitter``) are joined
from the live scheduler heap by ``db_id``.  On heap failure the DB rows are
returned without enrichment (degraded but functional; logged warning, no 500).

The heap snapshot is taken once — not per app — to avoid fan-out overhead.

parameters.name: source_tier

schema.description: Filter by source tier. 'app' excludes framework internals. 'framework' returns only internal actors. 'all' returns everything.
schema.title: Source Tier

parameters.description: Filter by source tier. 'app' excludes framework internals. 'framework' returns only internal actors. 'all' returns everything.

schema.title: Response All Jobs Api Scheduler Jobs Get

post.description:
Manually trigger a scheduled job to run immediately.

Looks up the job on the live scheduler heap by ``job_id`` (the job's ``db_id``). Returns
202 and dispatches the job through the same ``run_job_with_guard()`` path as a scheduled
fire, recording the execution with ``trigger_mode="manual"``. Returns 409 when the job
is not currently triggerable (already fired, mid-execution from its scheduled fire, or its
owning app is not running) or when a ``SINGLE``-mode job is currently executing.

A still-pending one-shot job (``After``/``Once`` trigger) is dequeued from the heap
before dispatch to prevent a second scheduled fire at its original time.

409.description: Job is not currently triggerable or is already executing

ActivityBucket.title: ActivityBucket
ActivityBucket.description: A single time-window bucket for the sparkline chart.

ActivityFeedEntry.title: ActivityFeedEntry
ActivityFeedEntry.description: A single activity entry for the cross-app recent activity feed.

AppHealthResponse.title: AppHealthResponse
AppHealthResponse.description: Health metrics for a single app instance.

recent_invocations_1h.title: Recent Invocations 1H
recent_invocations_1h.description: Total handler invocations in the last hour across all instances.

BackpressurePolicy.title: BackpressurePolicy
BackpressurePolicy.description:
What a listener does when the *global* dispatch semaphore is saturated.

The semaphore is shared by all listeners across all apps — saturation means
the whole bus is at capacity, not that this listener alone is busy. This is
distinct from per-listener rate controls (``debounce``, ``throttle``, ``mode``),
which operate inside the handler invoker after a dispatch slot is acquired.

BootIssueResponse.title: BootIssueResponse
BootIssueResponse.description: A boot-time issue entry in the system status response.

ConfigSchemaResponse.title: ConfigSchemaResponse
ConfigSchemaResponse.description:
Complete Hassette configuration as a JSON schema plus current values.

``config_schema`` is the fully-inlined JSON schema (all ``$ref``/``$defs`` resolved)
derived from ``HassetteConfig.model_json_schema()``.  ``config_values`` is the current
configuration serialized to JSON with ``SecretStr`` fields replaced by a masked
placeholder.  Every field and nested group is present — nothing is omitted.

DashboardAppGridEntry.title: DashboardAppGridEntry
DashboardAppGridEntry.description: Per-app health entry for the dashboard grid.

DashboardAppGridResponse.title: DashboardAppGridResponse
DashboardAppGridResponse.description: Dashboard app grid with per-app health data.

Execution.title: Execution
Execution.description:
Unified execution record returned by queries against the ``executions`` table.

Replaces the split ``HandlerInvocation`` / ``JobExecution`` models.
``kind`` discriminates between handler invocations and job executions.
Handler-only fields (``trigger_context_id``, ``trigger_origin``) default to
``None`` for job executions.

ExecutionMode.title: ExecutionMode
ExecutionMode.description: Overlap behavior for a listener when a trigger fires while a prior invocation still runs.

ExecutionStatus.title: ExecutionStatus
ExecutionStatus.description:
Status values for handler invocations and job executions.

Covers all values allowed by the CHECK constraints in migrations 001 and 005.
Pydantic v2 coerces plain strings to enum members on construction and
serialises back to plain strings in JSON responses.

JobSummary.title: JobSummary
JobSummary.description:
Per-job summary returned by ``get_job_summary()``.

``failed`` counts only ``'error'`` status; ``timed_out``, ``cancelled``, and ``skipped``
are tracked separately.
Invariant: ``successful + failed + cancelled + timed_out + skipped == total_executions``.

JobTriggerResponse.title: JobTriggerResponse
JobTriggerResponse.description:
Response for POST /api/scheduler/jobs/{job_id}/trigger.

Separate from ``ActionResponse`` (which has ``app_key``/``action`` but no ``job_id``) —
the trigger response identifies the job, not an app action.

ListenerWithSummary.title: ListenerWithSummary
ListenerWithSummary.description: Listener metrics enriched with human-readable handler summary.

ServiceInfoResponse.title: ServiceInfoResponse
ServiceInfoResponse.description: Structured info for one internal service.

TelemetryStatusResponse.title: TelemetryStatusResponse
TelemetryStatusResponse.description: Health check response for the telemetry database.
